meta.purpose: Selectable supplement catalog for the onboarding picker. Users choose their own supplement, form, dose and timing — the app never prescribes.
meta.source: Coimbra Protocol community materials shared by a family member (2026-07): FAQs Coimbraprotokoll, Patientenleitfaden 2019 (Prof. Dr. C.G. Coimbra), Magnesium notes, and 'NEM Infos und Produktempfehlungen' (© Christina Kiening, coimbraprotokoll.de).
meta.attribution: Compiled from the German Coimbra Protocol patient community. Product/form guidance © Christina Kiening (coimbraprotokoll.de). Shared for the app by a family member.
meta.disclaimer: Reference only — NOT medical advice. Individual doses (especially Vitamin D) are set by a trained protocol doctor based on labs. Never take >10,000 IU/day Vitamin D long-term without medical monitoring. Users enter their own regimen.

supplements.name: Vitamin D3 (Cholecalciferol)
supplements.notes: Individual dose set by the protocol doctor. WARNING: never >10,000 IU/day long-term without a doctor + blood/urine monitoring. Often split ~50/50 across breakfast & lunch. Drops go on a spoon (not in liquid), kept refrigerated & dark; capsules/softgels need no refrigeration.

supplements.name: Magnesium
supplements.role: Cofactor #1 — >300 enzyme processes, activates Vitamin D, calms nerves
supplements.notes: Dr. Coimbra: 800–1200 mg ELEMENTAL/day (label mg usually = raw compound, not elemental). Form is secondary to elemental total + tolerance. Citrate/oxide can cause loose stools; bisglycinate gentler/calming; L-Threonate crosses the blood-brain barrier (favored for MS, ~60% less needed). Water-soluble → overdose flushes out (may loosen stools). LDN users may go higher (~1000 mg).

supplements.name: Vitamin B2 (Riboflavin)
supplements.role: Cofactor — needed to convert Vitamin D; split into small doses
supplements.notes: Coimbra default ~240 mg/day Riboflavin, better split into several small doses. Turns urine bright yellow (harmless). MTHFR patients use the active form (Riboflavin-5-Phosphate; ~25 mg ≈ 100 mg plain riboflavin).

supplements.name: Vitamin B9 (Folate)
supplements.role: Cofactor — nerve/brain tissue, immune & DNA regulation
supplements.notes: Coimbra default 4–12 mg/day. Use natural/active folate (L-Methylfolate) — NOT synthetic folic acid, which can block folate receptors. High methyl-donor doses can cause jitters/headache in some.

supplements.name: Vitamin B12
supplements.notes: Coimbra default ~20 mcg/day. Prefer active methylcobalamin, especially for MTHFR patients.

supplements.name: B-Complex (full spectrum)
supplements.role: Cofactor — B-vitamins support each other; convenient alternative to individual B's
supplements.notes: MTHFR patients need the active/methylated forms. A B-complex can replace separate B2/B9/B12.

supplements.name: Choline
supplements.role: Cofactor — liver/detox; active form aids brain & myelin repair
supplements.notes: Coimbra default ~500 mg/day. CDP-Choline (Citicoline) additionally supports brain/nerve-cell healing (myelin synthesis).

supplements.name: Omega-3 (DHA/EPA)
supplements.role: Cofactor — anti-inflammatory, myelin synthesis (esp. year 1 of MS)
supplements.notes: Coimbra (MS): ~2,000 mg DHA/day. Prefer purified/molecular-processed oil (heavy-metal risk). Never heat it (heat-sensitive). Vegan = algae; flax/ALA is NOT sufficient. Store liquid oil refrigerated.

supplements.name: Vitamin K2
supplements.notes: Added by Dr. Coimbra in 2023 for bone density. Taken with breakfast alongside Vitamin D (fat-soluble).

supplements.name: Silicon (Silizium)
supplements.notes: Added by Dr. Coimbra in 2023 for bone density.

supplements.name: Cofactors combo capsule (e.g. 'Cofactors Immun')
supplements.role: Convenience — all cofactors except Magnesium & Omega-3 in one capsule
supplements.notes: Combines B-vitamins, folate, selenium, zinc, chromium, choline etc. Dose 3–6 caps/day by BMI/labs. Replaces the individual cofactors (still take Magnesium & Omega-3 separately).
